sanskrit: चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः। तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम्॥
transliteration: cātur-varṇyaṃ mayā sṛṣṭaṃ guṇa-karma-vibhāgaśaḥ | tasya kartāram api māṃ viddhy akartāram avyayam ||
literal_meaning: The four-fold order was created by Me according to qualities and actions; though its author, know Me as the non-doer, immutable.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that every social role is a temporary costume tailored to one’s inner guna-profile, not a fixed identity; the Self (the ‘I’ behind the role) remains untouched by any performance.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance resolved: Arjuna sees that his warrior identity is situational, not existential, reducing role-anxiety.
trigger_scenario: A mid-career professional dreads Monday meetings because ‘I’m not a salesman at heart’; realizing the job is just a guna-based function, not the Self, the dread loosens.
anchor_text: Play your role, but remember you are not the role.
